column17:
Kit Overview: Weakness Implanting Erudition (Sub) DPS but prefer low targets fight
Each hit from Anaxa's attacks inflicts one weakness on enemy, based on the amount of weakness, he will ignore part of DEF, when enemies have at least 5 weaknesses, they are inflicted with [Qualitative Disclosure] and Anaxa deal increased DMG to them, attacking enemy with BA or Skill in this state will unleash an additional usage of skill and does not consume Skill Point.

Anaxa's skill will deal 5 instances of bouncing attack. The subsequent bounce will prioritize hitting enemies who haven't been hit by his skill. Anaxa's ultimate deals AoE damage, implants all weaknesses prevent them from taking action.

If Anaxa is the sole Erudition unit in the team, he gains a huge amount of CRIT DMG, otherwise, he buffs the whole team's DMG%

Synergies:
✦ Units able to take advantage of his Weaknesses implant
✦ Hypercarry or Dual DPS support units depending on the team.

Notes:
✦ Weakness implant does not mean the target's Type RES is removed.

Tips:
✦ If the enemy is already inflicted with [Qualitative Disclosure], Anaxa can use BA to save Skill point.
✦ In teams where attack frequency matters more, Anaxa can use ERR% rope.
column45:
✦ If you like a character, get them!

✦ Anaxagoras is a very versatile DPS, as long as you have decent investment in his teams, he can tackle all kinds of content.

✦ While Anaxa is an Erudition unit, his performance is Pure Fiction or AoE content is not outstanding, but he can works fine. He is best in situations where there are fewer enemies such as Memories of Chaos or especially Apocalyptic Shadow, where his weakness implant can be extremely impactful
column71:
Can work with various Harmony and Nihility
Function well with other Erudition or Hunt characters. 
Can be used in any hypercarry setups

column59: S1 ≥ E1 > E2 - Stop at S1 for low invest

S1 provides 10 energy per turn, guaranteeing 3-turn Ultimate without ERR% rope, 60% increase DMG to enemies who has a weakness implanted by the wearer and 12% DEF shred to attacked enemies

E1 recovers 1 Skill point after using skill for the first time and using skill will decrease hit enemies' DEF by 16%

E2 decrease enemies' All-Type Res by 20%, making Anaxa an effective support unit, when enemies enter the battlefield, automatically triggers 1 instance of the Talent's Weakness Implant effect.

column9: E spam when there's enough SP

column17:
Kit Overview: AoE Nuke DPS with Ultimate and Enhanced Skill
When The Herta joins the battle, enemies hit by allies get inflicted with 1 stack of [Interpretation], up to 42 stacks. For each enemy attacked, generates 3 fixed energy for The Herta. Each stack of [Interpretation], along with the effects she provides pairing with another Erudition ally, give her numerous self buffs to tackle a variety of battles.

Her Ultimate rearranges stacks of [Interpretation] and unleashes an attack, Advances Forward her by 100%, and enhances her next Skill. Her Enhanced Skill deals devastating damage and resets [Interpretation] stacks of the main target to 1.

Synergies:
✦ Erudition ally is a must to activate her team buff Trace and help stack [Interpretation], along with units that have frequent AoE damage instances.
✦ Supports that buff The Herta and her team's damage and energy, or provide Speed buffs/Action Advance to improve action frequency.
Notes:
✦ Since [Interpretation] stacks & The Herta's energy gained is based on the amount of enemies and how often they get attacked, her damage output is lowered in 1-3 targets fights.
✦ The Herta's teams can be Skill Point intensive, prioritize giving her SP to use skill.
Tips:
✦ Erudition allies can use equipments such as ERR% rope, 5% ERR% planar ornaments and the Passkey Light Cone to attack more often and recharge her energy.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ The Herta can deal a great amount of damage in all kind of situations, provided she can consistently build up [Interpretation] stacks. (5-target linked boss, boss with summons, Pure Fiction, etc.)

✦ The Herta has worse performance in pure ST fights.

✦ If wanting a reliable AoE DPS, The Herta would be a great pickup.

✦ Finally, her Technique provides great Quality-of-Life by clearing waves of enemies in Simulated Universe and detecting chests. It's useful for traversing both in SU and the overworld.
column71:
Allies that can attack multiple enemies often should be priority.
Erudition ally changes based on the enemies.
Both Team-wide and Hypercarry support can be used.

column59:
E1 increases Enhanced Skill DMG and reduces the time needed to stack up [Interpretation].

E2 makes Enhanced Skill Advance Forward The Herta by 35% and allows for a second Enhanced Skill per Ult, greatly improving the frequency of her explosive damage output.

S1 provides QoL for SP economy and a good DMG buff but there are many alternatives for Erudition LC.

The Herta E0S0 is solid, E2S0/S1 is great for spenders.

column17:
Kit Overview: Constant AoE Super Break DPS
Rappa's main focus is her Ultimate: enters [Sealform] state, enhances Basic Attack to deal Universal Toughness Reduction to all enemies (though attacking non Imaginary-weak only deals half the Toughness Reduction), and allows her to deal Super Break DMG once enemies are broken.

When allies Weakness Break an enemy, her talent gains a charge (store up to 10), which enables her to deal huge Break DMG, even in ST scenarios.

Synergies:
✦ Units that has Break-Damage related buff: Super Break enabler, WBE buff, DEF shred, etc.
✦ Supports and DPS units that helps with breaking enemies.

Notes:
✦ Rappa struggles against enemies immune to Toughness Reduction.
✦ Since Rappa does not implant Weakness, she would have a slower clear time if enemies' weakness don't match with Rappa or other teammates.

Tips:
✦ The first 2 hits of EBA is Blast, manage it well to distribute your Toughness Reduction more evenly.
✦ Imaginary Break DMG has a low multiplier, so allies of other types can handle breaking instead.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Rappa is bound to perform with low investment as long as she or her team can Weakness Break enemies, mainly in AOE fights.

✦ Prime example being Pure Fiction, where enemies abundant and have reduced Toughness value, or when bosses summon more enemies on the fields.

✦ In unfavorable situations, Rappa would require more vertical investment to clear comfortably.
column71:
Fugue / Dahlia is nearly fixed for more Super Break DMG proc.
Allies that can contribute to breaking are also beneficial for the battle.

column59:
Fugue's Exo-Toughness is highly appreciated by Rappa. Dahlia also provides huge amount of DMG increase, as well as weakness implant.

S1 grants energy to use Ultimate right away, gives AA% per 2 EBA, which boost her turn frequency greatly, making S1 is a strong priority.

E1 gives DEF ignore and lets you keep Ultimate uptime more comfortably, E2 increases your ST Toughness damage.

E0S1 Rappa is a good stop, afterwards, you should focus on her supports.

column9: Less frequent SP usage than average DPS
E spam until the game tell you to use A

column17:
Kit Overview: Powerful Area of Effect specialist.
Jade's Skill applies [Debt Collector] to an ally, granting 30 Speed and Additional Damage with no downtime at the cost of 2% of that ally's HP per their attack.

AoE FUA is launched at 8 Charges, which are gained per enemy hit by her and her [Debt Collector]'s attacks. Jade's FUAs do not count towards these Charges. Her Talent grants massive self-buffs, making her less reliant on supports. Ultimate deals respectable damage and enhances her next 2 FUAs.

Synergies:
✦ Frequent multi-target attacker to be made as the [Debt Collector].
✦ Support units who can buff the whole team, or ones who help generate Charges by Advancing her [Debt Collector].
✦Characters that value the HP drains

Note:
✦ Jade's performance is rather subpar in 1 or 2 targets situation.

Tips:
✦ Hypercarry Jade's [Debt Collector] should still be a unit with frequent AoE attacks, as the amount of Charges Jade generates on her own turn is low.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ As long as Jade can consistently gain her Charge,  she will be able to perform excellently in multi-target scenarios.

✦ More vertical investment might be needed for ST/low-target fights, but at that point, you might want to let other DPS do the job instead.
column71:
A very flexible addition to AoE Dual DPS comps.
E1 enables teams with a ST Debt Collector.

column59:
E1 Increase FUA's Damage, targeted ally now generates 3 Charges per attack minimum.

Since you normally don't bring Jade to a pure ST fight, Lingsha being a constant AOE sustain character will be a good [Debt Collector] for her, thus making E1 only worth if you want to explore Jade's viability with ST units or battles.

S1 is a really strong stat boost.

Efficiency wise, Lingsha with E0S1 or E0S0 Jade is a strong combo.

column71: Jade with 2 supports (Sunday uses skill on Lingsha for stacks)
Jade with ST Debt Collector - E1 Only

column17:
Kit Overview: AoE specialized DPS
Argenti's Talent grant him energy whenever he attacks, the more enemies he hits, the more energy he gains. Each enemies hit will also grant him [Apotheosis] stacks, which increases his Crit Rate and Crit Damage.

Argenti has 180 max Energy. Argenti's Skill and Ultimate deals AoE damage to enemies. However his ultimate has 2 forms: 
- [For In This Garden Supreme Beauty Bestows]: Consumes 90 energy to unleash an AoE attack
- [Merit Bestowed in "My" Garden]: Consumes 180 energy to unleash an even a stronger AoE attack. He will also deals 6 additional instances of bounce damage afterwards.

Synergies:
✦ Supports that provide action advance, energy or good damage amplification
✦ Work as enabler for other erudition unit, such as with Jade, Mini Herta or The Herta.
Notes:
✦ Argenti's scaling on his fully charged Ultimate is pretty low, it's recommended to spam his 90 cost Ultimate for finishing off minions or small mobs.
✦ ERR% rope can be used on Pure Fiction build
column45:
✦ If you like the character, pull them!

✦ Argenti has always been a great DPS option in Pure Fiction, he is a great pull if you lack good DPS for this specific content. However his performance in other content may not be as great due to mediocre scalings.
column71: Basically any hypercarry setup works for him, prioritize energy regen buff

column59:
S1 offer dmg% on Ult and CDMG and E1 offer CDMG

His early Eidolon isn't that good and his premium support offer much more compared to his Eidolon, thus investing more to get his premium support is recommended

column17:
Kit Overview: Versatile FuA DPS
Jing Yuan's talent summons [Lightning Lord] (LL) in the action order bar, which is a 3-target bounce FUA that gain more speed and hit per action after using skill and ultimate attack. This allows him to be good or great in all kind of scenarios rather than just an AoE specialist.

Synergies:
✦ Advance Forward, Speed, and Energy buffer to help Jing Yuan gain more LL stack
✦ Crit DPS supports in general. 

Notes:
✦ [Lightning Lord] can not act when Jing Yuan is affected by crowd-control, so cleanse or CC-prevention is highly appreciated for him.
✦ [Lightning Lord] being a Bounce type attack can make it harder to attempt a flawless run, though it's not a problem in general.

Tips:
✦ You can skip on using his technique to buy time for Jing Yuan to kill mobs using his ult and skill, so LL can focus on boss or elite (except when paired with Sunday).
✦ Although it's great if you can get 10 stack on LL, getting 8 stack is a minimal dps loss.
✦ Jing Yuan damage output is closely split between Skill + Ultimate and LL, so abilities that don't buff his full kit (Like non E6 Bronya's skill, Birth of Self Lighcone, etc) are discouraged.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Being a versatile DPS as mentioned, Jing Yuan is a good choice if you want to expand your DPS selection, he is bound to perform decently across all game mode as long as you give him a good team.
column71: He excels in Hyper Carry teams

column59:
Sunday being able to directly advance summons will buff Light Lord - the most notable part of Jing Yuan's kit - makes him a high priority.

S1 offers stronger damage increase than Eidolons with added benefit of being useful for most Crit Erudition unit.

Sunday with E0S1 Jing Yuan is a good stop for vertical invest.

column71: Meme team - Xianzhou Luofu Prepares for battle

column17:
Kit Overview: FUA-orientated AoE DPS
Himeko's Skill does Blast damage, and deals more damage if the enemy is afflicted with Burn. Every attack of hers has a chance to inflict Burn.
Her Ultimate deals AoE damage to all enemies, and more energy is refunded to this Ult if any enemy is defeated.

The main premise of Himeko is her Talent. This Talent tallies up to 3 Charges, gains 1 when the battle starts, 1 when a common enemy has its Weakness Broken, and 3 if an Elite or Boss gets Weakness Broken.
Upon obtaining 3 Charges, Himeko launches a FUA that deals AoE damage, and then clears all Charges.

Synergies:
✦ Most of Himeko's damage comes from her Talent, allies that can assist with breaking faster/obtain more breaks is invaluable.
✦ Her AoE-damage FUA attack makes Himeko a good driver for other characters such as Herta, Jade, etc.
Notes:
✦ Try to manage Himeko's Charge to avoid wasting Charges since she can only hold 3 Charges. In battle, try to break Elite/Boss first.
✦ Himeko's damage against a single target is pitiful, she's mostly good for AoE situations such as Pure Fiction.
column45:
✦ If you like the character, get them!

✦ Himeko can be obtained from Standard banner or from losing 50/50 so you do not need to specifically aim to get her.
column71: Typical Hypercarry or Dual DPS team, ensures she can trigger FUA often

column59: Double the amount of breaks for Himeko simply means double the amounts of Talent activation.
